prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python Flask application using Python 3.12 slim base image. Install flask==3.0.0 and gunicorn==21.2.0 from requirements.txt. Expose port 5000 and run with gunicorn binding to 0.0.0.0:5000.
common_failures: Using python:latest instead of pinned version
Missing EXPOSE instruction

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application using node:20-alpine base image. Copy package.json and package-lock.json, run npm ci --only=production, copy source code, expose port 3000, and start with node server.js.
common_failures: Using npm install instead of npm ci
Copying all files before package.json (breaks layer caching)

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Go application that compiles a static binary. Use golang:1.22-alpine to build, set CGO_ENABLED=0 and GOOS=linux, and run go build -o /app/server ./cmd/server. The final CMD should run /app/server.
common_failures: Missing CGO_ENABLED=0 for static binary
Wrong go build output path

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Java Spring Boot application using eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine as base. Copy the pre-built JAR file app.jar into the container and run it with java -jar /app/app.jar with JVM flags -Xmx512m -Xms256m.
common_failures: Using JDK instead of JRE for runtime
Missing JVM memory flags

prompt: Write a Dockerfile to serve a static website using nginx:1.27-alpine. Copy static files from ./dist to /usr/share/nginx/html. Copy a custom nginx.conf to /etc/nginx/nginx.conf. Expose port 80.
common_failures: Using nginx:latest
Missing custom nginx.conf for production tuning

prompt: Write a Dockerfile based on alpine:3.19 that installs curl, jq, and bash using apk add --no-cache. Set the working directory to /app and copy a script entrypoint.sh. Run chmod +x and set it as the entrypoint.
common_failures: Missing --no-cache in apk add (increases image size)
Separate RUN for each apk install

prompt: Write a Dockerfile based on ubuntu:22.04 that installs python3, python3-pip, and libpq-dev using apt-get. Clean up apt cache in the same RUN layer. Set DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive.
common_failures: Separate RUN layers for apt-get update and install (breaks cache)
Not cleaning apt cache in same layer

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Ruby on Rails application using ruby:3.3-slim. Install bundler, copy Gemfile and Gemfile.lock, run bundle install --without development test, copy app code, expose port 3000, and start with bundle exec rails server -b 0.0.0.0.
common_failures: Installing dev/test gems in production image
Missing --without development test

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a PHP Laravel application using php:8.3-fpm-alpine. Install composer, copy composer.json and composer.lock, run composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader, copy application source, and expose port 9000.
common_failures: Installing dev dependencies in production
Missing --optimize-autoloader for production

prompt: Write a Dockerfile based on bash:5.2 that copies a shell script run.sh into /usr/local/bin, makes it executable with chmod +x, and sets it as the default CMD. Set a WORKDIR of /workspace.
common_failures: Script not executable
Missing WORKDIR instruction

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for a Python application: build stage uses python:3.12-slim to install dependencies from requirements.txt into /install using pip install --prefix=/install; runtime stage copies /install from build stage and runs the app. This reduces final image size.
common_failures: COPY --from stage name mismatch
Not copying /install directory to runtime stage

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for Node.js: build stage uses node:20-alpine, runs npm ci and npm run build to produce /app/dist; production stage uses node:20-alpine, copies only /app/dist and package.json, runs npm ci --only=production, and starts with node dist/index.js.
common_failures: Copying node_modules from build stage (defeats the purpose)
Missing npm ci --only=production in final stage

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for Go: builder stage uses golang:1.22-alpine with CGO_ENABLED=0 to build a statically linked binary; final stage uses scratch (empty base) and copies only the compiled binary. Set the binary as ENTRYPOINT.
common_failures: Using non-static build with scratch base (missing libc)
Forgetting to copy CA certificates for HTTPS in scratch image

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for Java: build stage uses maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21-alpine to run mvn package -DskipTests producing target/app.jar; runtime stage uses eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine and copies only the JAR. Run with java -jar.
common_failures: Using JDK image in runtime stage
Copying entire target/ directory instead of just the JAR

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile: build stage uses node:20-alpine to run npm ci and npm run build, producing a React SPA in /app/build; production stage uses nginx:1.27-alpine and serves the built artifacts from /usr/share/nginx/html with a custom nginx.conf.
common_failures: Copying node_modules into nginx stage
Wrong build output path for create-react-app vs vite

prompt: Write a three-stage Dockerfile for Python: test stage runs pytest to validate code, build stage installs production dependencies, runtime stage runs the application. Ensure tests run before build, and runtime does not include test dependencies.
common_failures: Test dependencies leaking into runtime stage
Tests not actually running (RUN pytest not present)

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for a .NET 8 application: build stage uses mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0-alpine to dotnet publish; runtime stage uses mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0-alpine copying published output. Expose port 8080.
common_failures: Using SDK image as runtime (much larger)
Missing --configuration Release in dotnet publish

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for a Rust application: builder stage uses rust:1.77-alpine running cargo build --release; final stage uses alpine:3.19 copying only the compiled binary. Install required shared libraries (musl-dev) in builder.
common_failures: Missing musl target for static linking on Alpine
Not using --release flag in cargo build

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for a Ruby app with asset compilation: build stage uses ruby:3.3-slim to install gems and run asset precompilation (rake assets:precompile); runtime stage copies gems and compiled assets, excludes dev/test groups.
common_failures: Asset compilation tools not available in runtime stage
Node.js not installed for asset compilation

prompt: Write a two-stage Dockerfile for PHP with Composer: build stage uses composer:2.7 to install dependencies with --no-dev --optimize-autoloader; runtime stage uses php:8.3-fpm-alpine copying vendor/ from build stage. Expose port 9000.
common_failures: Copying entire build stage instead of just vendor/
Missing --optimize-autoloader

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python application optimized for layer caching: first copy only requirements.txt and install dependencies, then copy source code. Use python:3.12-slim, set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Copying source before requirements.txt (breaks dependency caching)
Missing PYTHONUNBUFFERED for proper logging

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for Node.js with dev and prod build targets using ARG TARGET=production. In development: install all deps, mount source. In production: multi-stage with only prod deps and built artifacts. Use node:20-alpine. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: ARG value not passed to build correctly
Dev stage including production secrets

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a GPU-enabled Python ML application using nvidia/cuda:12.3.0-runtime-ubuntu22.04 as base. Install PyTorch 2.2.0 with CUDA 12.1 support. Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and configure the container for GPU access. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Using cuda:devel instead of cuda:runtime (much larger)
PyTorch CUDA version mismatch with base image

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-platform Go service that builds for both linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. Use BUILDPLATFORM and TARGETPLATFORM ARGs with golang:1.22 base. Cross-compile using GOOS and GOARCH from TARGETPLATFORM. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Not using --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM for build stage
GOARCH/GOOS not set from TARGETPLATFORM ARG

prompt: Write a Dockerfile that uses RUN --mount=type=secret to inject an API key at build time without it appearing in image layers. The build stage fetches a private Python package using the secret. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Using ENV or ARG for secrets (leaked in image history)
Secret not mounted correctly with id=

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python Flask API with a HEALTHCHECK instruction that hits GET /health every 30 seconds with 10 second timeout, 3 retries, and 5 second start period. Use python:3.12-slim and run with gunicorn. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Missing --start-period for slow-starting apps
Using wget instead of curl when curl not installed

prompt: Write a Dockerfile that installs a custom corporate CA certificate (ca-cert.crt) into the system trust store during the build. Base on python:3.12-slim. Configure pip to use the CA cert for private package registry access. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Using pip install --trusted-host instead of proper CA cert
CA cert not copied before update-ca-certificates

prompt: Write a Dockerfile that creates a non-root user with specific UID 1001 and GID 1001 named appuser, sets file ownership of /app, and runs the application as this user. Use node:20-alpine base. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Creating user without setting ownership of working directory
Using numeric UID without creating named user

prompt: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python app with build ARGs for VERSION and BUILD_DATE. The build stage accepts these ARGs and bakes them into the image as LABEL metadata (OCI image labels). Use python:3.12-slim. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: ARG declared after FROM (not accessible before FROM)
Missing OCI standard label keys

prompt: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python app with C extensions (like numpy/scipy): build stage uses python:3.12-slim with gcc and build tools to compile extensions; runtime stage uses python:3.12-slim with only runtime libraries. Apply a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Build tools (gcc) present in runtime image
Missing runtime library dependencies (libgomp for OpenMP)

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python application using gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12 as the final runtime image (no shell, no package manager). Multi-stage: build with python:3.12-slim, install deps to /install prefix, then COPY --from into the distroless image. Run as non-root.
common_failures: Shell commands in distroless image (no /bin/sh)
Python path not configured for /install prefix

prompt: Write a Dockerfile that creates and runs as a non-root user with UID 1000 and GID 1000. The application directory /app must be owned by this user. Base on node:20-alpine. Explicitly set USER 1000:1000 before the CMD instruction.
common_failures: Running as UID 0 (root) by default
File ownership not set before USER switch

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python API designed for read-only filesystem operation: mount /tmp and /var/run as tmpfs in Docker Compose (document in comments). Use python:3.12-slim, create non-root user, ensure the app writes only to /tmp. Add VOLUME declarations for writable paths.
common_failures: Application writing to locations outside tmpfs mounts
Missing VOLUME for writable directories

prompt: Write a hardened Dockerfile that explicitly drops all Linux capabilities and prevents privilege escalation. Use a minimal base (alpine:3.19), install only needed packages, set USER to non-root. Include a security.txt LABEL noting no-new-privileges. Document docker run --cap-drop=ALL --no-new-privileges usage.
common_failures: User capabilities documented but not enforced in Dockerfile
Using privileged base image

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python service using a minimal attack surface: distroless base (gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12), no package manager in final image, no shell, dependencies copied from builder, non-root runtime. Include SBOM generation in a separate build target.
common_failures: Package manager present in distroless final image
SBOM target not using correct syft image

prompt: Write a Dockerfile with a dedicated sbom build target that runs anchore/syft to generate an SBOM in SPDX JSON format from the final image. The Dockerfile should have standard build and sbom targets. Use python:3.12-slim as the application base.
common_failures: syft not outputting to file (missing -o flag)
SBOM target not based on final image

prompt: Write a Dockerfile with a sign build target that uses cosign to sign the container image after build. Include a Makefile-style comment showing the cosign sign command with --key. The application base should be python:3.12-slim with non-root user and HEALTHCHECK.
common_failures: cosign sign step not referencing built image digest
Missing HEALTHCHECK in final image

prompt: Write a Dockerfile with a scan build target that runs truffleHog3 to detect secrets in the source code before building. The scan stage should fail the build if secrets are found. Final stage uses python:3.12-slim with non-root user.
common_failures: Scan stage not failing build on secret detection
truffleHog not scanning the correct directory

prompt: Write a Dockerfile demonstrating supply chain security: pin all base images with digest (sha256:...), use --mount=type=cache for package manager cache, generate provenance with buildkit metadata, and add OCI image spec labels including source and revision.
common_failures: Digest pinning not including tag (use image:tag@sha256:...)
Missing OCI source/revision labels

prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a rootless Podman-compatible container: do not use USER root at any point, use FROM ubi9/ubi-minimal:9.3 as base, set all directories to be owned by uid/gid 1001, use numeric USER 1001:1001, and ensure no setuid/setgid binaries. Include HEALTHCHECK.
common_failures: setuid/setgid binaries not removed
Using USER root even temporarily during build

prompt: Write a fully hardened nginx Dockerfile: multi-stage build, final stage FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 (non-root), copy pre-built nginx binary and config, read-only filesystem compatible (no writes at runtime), HEALTHCHECK with curl on /health, all OCI labels, pinned base image digest. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: distroless image missing required CA certs for HTTPS upstream
nginx config writing to /var/cache/nginx not allowed in read-only filesystem

prompt: Write a hardened Python API Dockerfile: multi-stage with builder (python:3.12-slim) and final (gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12), non-root user, SBOM generation stage using syft, vulnerability scan stage using grype blocking on CRITICAL CVEs, OCI labels, pinned digests, HEALTHCHECK. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: grype not configured to fail on CRITICAL (missing --fail-on critical)
SBOM not generated from final image layer

prompt: Write a Dockerfile designed for zero-CVE operation: use chainguard/python:3.12 as base (daily rebuilt, minimal attack surface), pin to digest, create non-root user, run vulnerability scan with grype as a separate build target that blocks on any HIGH or CRITICAL CVE. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Chainguard image tag drifting instead of pinned to digest
grype fail-on threshold too permissive

prompt: Write a Dockerfile using cgr.dev/chainguard/python as the final base with cosign image signing. Include: multi-stage build, digest-pinned base, SBOM via syft in spdx-json format, image signing step with cosign (keyless using OIDC), non-root user, HEALTHCHECK. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: cosign sign using key instead of keyless OIDC
syft generating SBOM from wrong stage

prompt: Write a complete CI/CD Dockerfile with four named stages: lint (runs flake8 and mypy), test (runs pytest with coverage), build (installs production dependencies), and final (distroless runtime). Each stage should be independently targetable. Use python:3.12-slim for non-final stages. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Stages not independently targetable with --target
Test coverage not enforced (missing --cov-fail-under)

prompt: Write a SLSA Level 3 compliant Dockerfile: digest-pinned base images, reproducible builds (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH set), build provenance metadata as OCI labels, no network access during build (--network=none where possible), hermetic build environment. Use golang:1.22-alpine. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Non-reproducible timestamps breaking SLSA provenance
Missing hermetic build network isolation documentation

prompt: Write a hardened multi-platform Go service Dockerfile targeting linux/amd64 and linux/arm64: BUILDPLATFORM/TARGETPLATFORM cross-compilation, gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 final stage with digest-pinned, non-root user (65532:65532 nonroot), HEALTHCHECK, OCI labels. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Cross-compilation GOARCH mismatch with TARGETPLATFORM
distroless nonroot UID not 65532

prompt: Write a PCI-DSS compliant Dockerfile: no secrets in ENV or ARG, all packages pinned to exact versions with hash verification, non-root UID 10001, read-only filesystem (VOLUME for writable dirs), HEALTHCHECK, disable core dumps (ulimit), audit logging to stdout. Base on python:3.12-slim. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: pip install without --require-hashes
Core dump not disabled (missing ulimit -c 0)

prompt: Write a production-grade multi-stage Dockerfile with all security best practices: digest-pinned base, multi-stage (lint, test, build, final-distroless), non-root user, HEALTHCHECK, SBOM stage, vulnerability scan stage (grype block on HIGH+), OCI labels, --mount=type=cache for pip, no secrets in layers. Use python:3.12-slim. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: Grype not blocking on HIGH severity
SBOM generated from build stage instead of final image

prompt: Write a hardened Java application Dockerfile: builder uses eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine for mvn package, final stage uses gcr.io/distroless/java21-debian12 (non-root nonroot user), JVM flags -XX:+UseContainerSupport -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0 -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError, HEALTHCHECK on /actuator/health, OCI labels, digest-pinned bases. Set a non-root USER with appropriate securityContext and Secret management.
common_failures: JVM flags not using container-aware memory settings
/actuator/health endpoint not enabled in Spring Boot config
